title: Schooling is rising in most regions
subtitle: Expected years of schooling by region
source: World Bank. 2026. Human Capital Index Plus.
visdescription: Line chart showing the rise in expected years of schooling between 2001 and 2025.
textParagraph: Since 2001, global access to education has expanded significantly, particularly in low- and middle-income economies. South Asia has recorded the largest gain, with expected years of schooling increasing from eight to eleven years. There has also been significant growth in Sub-Saharan Africa, where expected years of schooling rose from seven to almost nine years. The Middle East, North Africa, Afghanistan & Pakistan, East Asia & Pacific, and Latin America & the Caribbean all saw an increase of roughly one year over the period. In higher-income regions, like Europe & Central Asia and North America, where these systems reached close to universal completion in primary and secondary education, figures have remained relatively stable.
keyFactLabel: of schooling for the average child globally in 2024.
